models.description: Minimal Virtual Subsection (VSS) layout generation for ETCS Hybrid Level 3. Given a timetable, the model determines the minimum number of VSS borders that must be installed so that all trains can run conflict-free on a time-discretized railway network. It is a flexible formulation with several toggles: routes can be fixed (fixedRoutes) or part of the optimization (freeRoutes); VSS positions can be discretized into vertices (Discrete model) or kept as continuous positions on edges (Continuous / Inferred / InferredAlt models); train dynamics (acceleration/deceleration) and quadratic/PWL braking curves can optionally be included. Based on reference [3] (Engels, Peham, Wille, ATMOS 2023), with the iterative variant from [4] and precomputed-routing variant from [6]. Time is discretized into steps of length delta_t (default 15s).

sets_indices.name: T
sets_indices.meaning: discrete time steps t = 0..num_t (each step is delta_t seconds)

sets_indices.name: E_b
sets_indices.meaning: breakable edges (edges that may receive VSS borders)

sets_indices.name: E_rel
sets_indices.meaning: relevant edges (representative breakable edges, reverse edges combined) used for the VSS-count objective

sets_indices.name: S_ub
sets_indices.meaning: unbreakable sections (groups of edges that cannot be split by VSS)

sets_indices.name: V_nb
sets_indices.meaning: candidate VSS-border vertices in discretized model

sets_indices.name: VSS_e
sets_indices.meaning: index of potential VSS border on edge e, 0..max_vss_on_edge(e)-1

parameters.name: delta_t
parameters.meaning: length of a discretized time step in seconds

parameters.name: minblk_e
parameters.meaning: minimum block length on edge e

parameters.name: r_len_tr
parameters.meaning: total route length of train tr (fixed-routes variant)

parameters.name: ABS_PWL_ERROR
parameters.meaning: maximal error for piecewise-linear braking approximation

decision_variables.name: v_{tr,t}
decision_variables.meaning: speed of train tr at time step t

decision_variables.name: x_{tr,t,e}
decision_variables.meaning: 1 if train tr occupies edge e at time t

decision_variables.name: x_sec_{tr,t,s}
decision_variables.meaning: 1 if train tr occupies unbreakable section s at time t

decision_variables.name: y_sec_fwd_{t,i}
decision_variables.meaning: 1 if fwd/bwd section i is occupied in forward direction at time t

decision_variables.name: y_sec_bwd_{t,i}
decision_variables.meaning: 1 if fwd/bwd section i is occupied in backward direction at time t

decision_variables.name: b_{i}
decision_variables.meaning: DISCRETE model: 1 if candidate vertex i is used as a VSS border (objective term)

decision_variables.name: b_used_{e,vss}
decision_variables.meaning: CONTINUOUS model: 1 if the vss-th potential border on edge e is used (objective term)

decision_variables.name: b_pos_{e,vss}
decision_variables.meaning: CONTINUOUS/Inferred model: position of the vss-th VSS border on edge e

decision_variables.name: b_front_{tr,t,e,vss}
decision_variables.meaning: 1 if the front of train tr is in front of border vss on edge e at time t

decision_variables.name: b_rear_{tr,t,e,vss}
decision_variables.meaning: 1 if the rear of train tr is in front of border vss on edge e at time t (only for trains with train-integrity monitoring)

decision_variables.name: num_vss_segments_{e}
decision_variables.meaning: INFERRED model: number of VSS segments on edge e (objective uses num_vss_segments - 1)

decision_variables.name: edge_type_{e,k}
decision_variables.meaning: INFERRED model: 1 if separation function k is used on edge e

decision_variables.name: type_num_vss_segments_{e,k,vss}
decision_variables.meaning: INFERRED_ALT model: 1 if edge e uses separation function k with vss+1 segments

decision_variables.name: frac_vss_segments / frac_type
decision_variables.meaning: INFERRED model: fractional positions of borders given the separation function (auxiliary)

decision_variables.name: brakelen_{tr,t}
decision_variables.meaning: braking distance of train tr at time t (only if include_braking_curves)

decision_variables.name: mu_{tr,t}
decision_variables.meaning: FIXED-ROUTES: position of train front along its route at time t

decision_variables.name: lda_{tr,t}
decision_variables.meaning: FIXED-ROUTES: position of train rear (lambda) along its route at time t

decision_variables.name: x_mu_{tr,t,e}, x_lda_{tr,t,e}
decision_variables.meaning: FIXED-ROUTES: 1 if front/rear is past the start of edge e (used to derive x = x_mu AND x_lda)

decision_variables.name: e_mu_{tr,t,e}, e_lda_{tr,t,e}, overlap_{tr,t,e}, x_v_{tr,t,v}, len_in/len_out/x_in/x_out_{tr,t}
decision_variables.meaning: FREE-ROUTES auxiliary position/overlap/vertex-occupation variables (routing is part of optimization)

decision_variables.name: stopped_{tr,t}, b_tight_{tr,t,e,vss}, e_tight_{tr,t,e}
decision_variables.meaning: only-stop-at-VSS option: train-stopped indicator and tight-border indicators

objective.expression_latex: \min \; \begin{cases} \sum_{i \in V_{nb}} b_i & \text{(Discrete)} \\ \sum_{e \in E_{rel}} \sum_{vss} b^{used}_{e,vss} & \text{(Continuous)} \\ \sum_{e \in E_{rel}} (\text{num\_vss\_segments}_e - 1) & \text{(Inferred)} \\ \sum_{e \in E_{rel}} \sum_{vss} (vss+1)\, \text{type\_num\_vss\_segments}_{e,*,vss} & \text{(InferredAlt)} \end{cases}
objective.expression_plain: minimize the total number of installed VSS borders/segments; the exact form depends on the chosen VSS model type (discrete vertex count, continuous border count, or inferred segment count)

constraints.name: vss_separation_discretized
constraints.indexed_over: no-border section, train pairs (tr1,tr2), time t, edge positions e1!=e2
constraints.expression_latex: 2 - x_{tr1,t,e1} - x_{tr2,t,e2} + \sum_{v \in overlap(e1,e2)} b_v \;\geq\; 1
constraints.expression_plain: DISCRETE model: two trains simultaneously on the same no-border section must be separated by at least one chosen VSS border vertex between them

constraints.name: unbreakable_section_occupation
constraints.indexed_over: unbreakable section s, train tr, time t
constraints.expression_latex: \sum_{e \in s} x_{tr,t,e} \geq x^{sec}_{tr,t,s}, \quad \sum_{e \in s} x_{tr,t,e} \leq |s|\, x^{sec}_{tr,t,s}, \quad \sum_{tr} x^{sec}_{tr,t,s} \leq 1
constraints.expression_plain: links section-occupation variable to edge occupation, and allows at most one train per unbreakable section at any time

constraints.name: schedule_station_constraints
constraints.indexed_over: train tr, stop, time t in [arrival,departure]
constraints.expression_latex: v_{tr,t} = 0;\quad x_{tr,t,e}=0 \;\forall e \notin \text{stop edges};\quad \sum_{e \in \text{stop edges}} x_{tr,t,e} \geq 1
constraints.expression_plain: while a train dwells at a scheduled stop its speed is zero, it occupies only station edges, and it occupies at least one station edge

constraints.name: speed_acceleration_limits
constraints.indexed_over: train tr, time t
constraints.expression_latex: v_{tr,t+1} - v_{tr,t} \leq a_{tr}\,\Delta t, \qquad v_{tr,t} - v_{tr,t+1} \leq d_{tr}\,\Delta t
constraints.expression_plain: limit acceleration and deceleration between consecutive time steps (only if include_train_dynamics)

constraints.name: edge_max_speed
constraints.indexed_over: train tr, edge e with vmax_e < vmax_tr, time t
constraints.expression_latex: v_{tr,t+1} \leq vmax_e + (vmax_{tr} - vmax_e)(1 - x_{tr,t,e})
constraints.expression_plain: speed must not exceed the edge speed limit while the train occupies that edge (big-M relaxed by x)

constraints.name: boundary_speed
constraints.indexed_over: train tr
constraints.expression_latex: v_{tr, t^{first}} = v0_{tr}, \qquad v_{tr, t^{last}+1} = vn_{tr}
constraints.expression_plain: fix the initial and final speed of each train

constraints.name: reverse_occupation_one_direction
constraints.indexed_over: fwd/bwd section i, time t
constraints.expression_latex: y^{fwd}_{t,i} + y^{bwd}_{t,i} \leq 1, \quad y^{fwd}_{t,i} \geq x_{tr,t,e}\;(e \in fwd_i), \; y^{fwd}_{t,i} \leq \sum x_{tr,t,e}
constraints.expression_plain: a breakable section may be occupied in only one travel direction at a time; reversing trains are not modelled

constraints.name: brakelen_quadratic_or_pwl
constraints.indexed_over: train tr, time t
constraints.expression_latex: brakelen_{tr,t} = \frac{v_{tr,t+1}^2}{2 d_{tr}}
constraints.expression_plain: braking distance equals the kinematic stopping distance at the next-step speed; modelled as a Gurobi quadratic constraint (addQConstr) or a piecewise-linear approximation (addGenConstrPWL with max error ABS_PWL_ERROR) when use_pwl

constraints.name: continuous_vss_used_position_link
constraints.indexed_over: relevant edge e, border vss
constraints.expression_latex: len_e\, b^{used}_{e,vss} \geq b^{pos}_{e,vss} \geq minblk_e\, b^{used}_{e,vss}; \quad b^{pos}_{e,vss} \geq b^{pos}_{e,vss+1} + minblk_e\, b^{used}_{e,vss+1}
constraints.expression_plain: CONTINUOUS model: a border position is non-zero only if the border is used, respecting the minimum block length, and border positions are ordered decreasing to remove symmetric solutions

constraints.name: border_front_rear_consistency
constraints.indexed_over: breakable edge e, train tr, time t, border vss
constraints.expression_latex: x_{tr,t,e} \geq b^{front}_{tr,t,e,vss},\; x_{tr,t,e} \geq b^{rear}_{tr,t,e,vss};\quad \sum_{tr,vss} b^{front} \geq \sum_{tr} x - 1; \quad \sum_{e,vss} b^{front}_{tr,t} \leq 1
constraints.expression_plain: non-discretized position logic: a border can only be used by a train present on the edge, enough borders must separate co-located trains, at most one border per train, front and rear borders must coincide, and a border is usable only if the VSS is used

constraints.name: inferred_fraction_pwl_constraints
constraints.indexed_over: relevant edge e, separation type k, border vss
constraints.expression_latex: \sum_k edge\_type_{e,k} = 1; \quad frac\_vss\_segments_{e,k,vss} = sep_k(vss, \text{num\_segments}); \quad b^{pos}_{e,vss} = len_e \sum_k frac\_type_{e,k,vss}
constraints.expression_plain: INFERRED model: choose exactly one separation function per edge, compute fractional border positions via a PWL of the chosen separation function, and linearize the product frac_type = frac_vss_segments * edge_type

constraints.name: inferred_alt_fraction_constraints
constraints.indexed_over: relevant edge e, separation type k, border vss
constraints.expression_latex: \sum_{k,vss} type\_num\_vss\_segments_{e,k,vss} \leq 1; \quad b^{pos}_{e,vss} = \sum_{k, n} type\_num\_vss\_segments_{e,k,n-1}\, len_e\, sep_k(vss,n+1)
constraints.expression_plain: INFERRED_ALT model: at most one (separation type, segment count) per edge sets all border positions directly

constraints.name: fixed_routes_position_dynamics
constraints.indexed_over: train tr, time t
constraints.expression_latex: mu_{tr,t} - lda_{tr,t} = len_{tr} + \tfrac{(v_{tr,t}+v_{tr,t+1})}{2}\Delta t \,(+\,brakelen_{tr,t}); \quad mu_{tr,t} - lda_{tr,t+1} = len_{tr}(+brakelen); \quad mu_{tr,t+1}\geq mu_{tr,t};\; lda_{tr,t+1}\geq lda_{tr,t}
constraints.expression_plain: FIXED-ROUTES: front-rear distance equals train length plus distance travelled (plus braking distance); positions are monotonically non-decreasing

constraints.name: fixed_routes_boundary
constraints.indexed_over: train tr
constraints.expression_latex: lda_{tr,t^{first}} = -len_{tr}, \qquad mu_{tr,t^{last}} = r\_len_{tr} + len_{tr} (+ brakelen)
constraints.expression_plain: FIXED-ROUTES: rear starts just before route entry, front ends just past route exit

constraints.name: fixed_routes_edge_occupation
constraints.indexed_over: train tr, route edge e, time t
constraints.expression_latex: x_{tr,t,e} = x^{lda}_{tr,t,e} \wedge x^{mu}_{tr,t,e}, \quad \text{with } x^{mu}=1 \Leftrightarrow mu_{tr,t} > pos^{start}_e,\; x^{lda}=1 \Leftrightarrow lda_{tr,t} < pos^{end}_e
constraints.expression_plain: FIXED-ROUTES: a train occupies an edge iff its front has passed the edge start and its rear has not passed the edge end (modelled with big-M indicator pairs and a Gurobi AND)

constraints.name: fixed_routes_station_position
constraints.indexed_over: train tr, stop
constraints.expression_latex: mu_{tr,t_0-1} \geq pos^{start}_{stop}, \; mu_{tr,t_1-1} \leq pos^{end}_{stop}, \; lda_{tr,t_0} \geq pos^{start}_{stop}, \; lda_{tr,t_1} \leq pos^{end}_{stop}
constraints.expression_plain: FIXED-ROUTES: front/rear positions confine the train to the station tracks during its dwell window

constraints.name: fixed_routes_impossibility_cuts
constraints.indexed_over: train tr, time t (only if use_schedule_cuts)
constraints.expression_latex: mu_{tr,t} \leq before\_max + \text{maxDist}(\cdot), \qquad lda_{tr,t} \geq after\_min - \text{maxDist}(\cdot)
constraints.expression_plain: FIXED-ROUTES: cutting planes bounding reachable positions from the previous/next known position using max travel distance (search-space reduction, not required for feasibility)

constraints.name: fixed_routes_common_entry_exit
constraints.indexed_over: trains sharing an entry/exit vertex
constraints.expression_latex: lda_{tr1,t} \geq 0 \;\text{(shared entry)}, \qquad mu_{tr1,t} \leq r\_len_{tr1}\;\text{(shared exit)}
constraints.expression_plain: FIXED-ROUTES: trains sharing an entry/exit point must not overlap there

constraints.name: fixed_routes_nondiscretized_border_position
constraints.indexed_over: train tr, route edge e, border vss
constraints.expression_latex: mu_{tr,t} - pos^{start}_e \leq b^{pos}_{e,vss} + M(1 - b^{front}_{tr,t,e,vss}); \quad lda_{tr,t} - pos^{start}_e + M(1-b^{rear}) \geq b^{pos}_{e,vss}
constraints.expression_plain: FIXED-ROUTES non-discretized: relate the continuous front/rear positions to the VSS border position via big-M when the train front/rear is in front of the border

constraints.name: free_routes_position_and_routing
constraints.indexed_over: train tr, time t, edge e, vertex v
constraints.expression_latex: len^{in}_{tr,t} + len^{out}_{tr,t} + \sum_e (e^{mu}_{tr,t,e} - e^{lda}_{tr,t,e}) = len_{tr} + \tfrac{(v_{tr,t}+v_{tr,t+1})}{2}\Delta t (+ brakelen); \quad \text{vertex/edge occupation linking } x_v, x
constraints.expression_plain: FREE-ROUTES: when routing is part of the optimization, per-edge position fragments (e_mu, e_lda), vertex-occupation (x_v) and in/out tail lengths jointly enforce train length, distance travelled, overlap and valid edge selection

constraints.name: only_stop_at_vss_constraints
constraints.indexed_over: train tr, time t, edge e, border vss (only if get_only_stop_at_vss)
constraints.expression_latex: \sum_{e,vss} b^{tight}_{tr,t,e,vss} \leq 1; \quad e^{tight}_{tr,t,e} + \sum_{vss} b^{tight}_{tr,t,e,vss} \geq x_{tr,t-1,e} - stopped_{tr,t}; \quad V_{MIN}\, stopped \leq v_{tr,t} \leq vmax_{tr}\, stopped
constraints.expression_plain: optional only-stop-at-VSS feature: a train may only come to a halt exactly at a VSS/TTD border; ties stopped indicator to speed and tight-border indicators

instances.name: example-networks (10 networks: SimpleStation, SimpleNetwork, SingleTrack, SingleTrackWithStation, Overtake, HighSpeedTrack2Trains, HighSpeedTrack5Trains, Stammstrecke4/8/16Trains)
instances.format: directory per instance with network/ (tracks.graphml, successors), routes/ (routes.json), timetable/ (trains.json, schedules.json, stations.json)

models.notes: Single flexible MILP with many compile-/run-time toggles assembled in create_variables (VSSGenTimetableSolver_general.cpp:1680-1705) and create_constraints (1707-1732). Branches: fix_routes -> fixedRoutes.cpp vs freeRoutes.cpp; vss_model type (Discrete -> discretized vertex variables b; Continuous/Inferred/InferredAlt -> non-discretized b_pos/b_used). include_train_dynamics toggles acceleration constraints; include_braking_curves toggles brakelen (quadratic addQConstr, or addGenConstrPWL if use_pwl). use_schedule_cuts adds impossibility cuts. The VSSGenTimetableSolverWithMovingBlockInformation subclass (VSSGenTimetableSolver_MovingBlockInformation.cpp) adds variable fixings/hints from a precomputed moving-block solution rather than new model structure. Big-M values are data-derived (mu_ub = r_len + tr_len + max_brakelen). Brakelen constraint and the Inferred PWL constraints make the full model an MIQCP/MINLP-with-PWL, not a pure MILP, depending on settings.

models.description: Optimal train routing and timetabling under moving-block train control. Trains are routed over a railway network with continuous velocity-extended arcs (each arc carries an entry and exit velocity discretization), and conflict-free running is enforced through headway constraints based on braking distances (moving authority), plus TTD-section and opposite-direction (reverse) safety constraints. The objective minimizes weighted total delay at network exit. Based on reference [5] (Engels, Wille, 'Comparing Lazy Constraint Selection Strategies in Train Routing with Moving Block Control', FedCSIS 2024, doi:10.15439/2024F3041, arXiv:2405.18977). Headway/TTD/reverse constraints may instead be generated on demand as Gurobi lazy constraints (GenPOMovingBlockMIPSolver_Lazy.cpp).

sets_indices.name: VE_tr,v
sets_indices.meaning: velocity extension set: discretized possible speeds of train tr at vertex v

sets_indices.name: RE
sets_indices.meaning: relevant reverse edge pairs (e1,e2) for opposite-direction collision avoidance

sets_indices.name: Stops_tr
sets_indices.meaning: scheduled stops of train tr with candidate stop vertices

parameters.name: tmin_n_tr
parameters.meaning: earliest desired exit (departure) time of train tr at its exit node

parameters.name: tmax_n_tr
parameters.meaning: latest allowed exit time of train tr; also serves as big-M upper bound for timing variables

parameters.name: t0_range_tr
parameters.meaning: [min,max] entry (arrival) time window of train tr

parameters.name: len_tr, vmax_tr, a_tr, d_tr
parameters.meaning: train length, max speed, acceleration, deceleration

parameters.name: min_t_arc / max_t_arc
parameters.meaning: min/max travel time over an arc at given entry/exit speeds, from kinematics (EOMHelper)

decision_variables.name: t_front_arrival_{tr,v}
decision_variables.meaning: time the front of train tr arrives at vertex v

decision_variables.name: t_front_departure_{tr,v}
decision_variables.meaning: time the front of train tr departs vertex v

decision_variables.name: t_rear_departure_{tr,v}
decision_variables.meaning: time the rear of train tr departs vertex v

decision_variables.name: t_ttd_departure_{tr,s}
decision_variables.meaning: time train tr fully leaves TTD section s

decision_variables.name: x_{tr,e}
decision_variables.meaning: 1 if train tr uses edge e

decision_variables.name: x_ttd_{tr,s}
decision_variables.meaning: 1 if train tr uses TTD section s

decision_variables.name: y_{tr,e,i,j}
decision_variables.meaning: 1 if train tr traverses edge e entering at velocity-extension i and leaving at velocity-extension j (only for kinematically feasible (i,j) per possible_by_eom)

decision_variables.name: order_{tr1,tr2,e}
decision_variables.meaning: 1 if tr1 immediately follows tr2 on edge e

decision_variables.name: order_ttd_{tr1,tr2,s}
decision_variables.meaning: 1 if tr1 follows tr2 through TTD section s

decision_variables.name: reverse_order_{tr1,tr2,idx}
decision_variables.meaning: 1 if tr1 traverses reverse-edge pair idx before tr2 (opposite directions)

decision_variables.name: stop_{tr,k,v}
decision_variables.meaning: 1 if train tr serves its k-th scheduled stop at vertex v

objective.expression_plain: minimize the weighted average delay, i.e. the train-weight-weighted sum of how much later than the earliest allowed exit time each train's rear actually leaves the network, normalized by the total weight

constraints.name: edge_velocity_extension_aggregation
constraints.indexed_over: train tr, edge e used by tr
constraints.expression_latex: x_{tr,e} = \sum_{(i,j) \text{ feasible}} y_{tr,e,i,j}
constraints.expression_plain: an edge is used iff exactly one of its kinematically feasible (entry-velocity, exit-velocity) arcs is selected

constraints.name: path_entry_exit_flow
constraints.indexed_over: train tr, vertex v
constraints.expression_latex: \sum_{e \in \delta^{out}(entry)} x_{tr,e} = 1; \quad \sum_{e \in \delta^{in}(exit)} x_{tr,e} = 1; \quad \sum_{e \in \delta^{in}(v)} x \leq 1,\; \sum_{e \in \delta^{out}(v)} x \leq 1
constraints.expression_plain: each train leaves its entry vertex exactly once, enters its exit vertex exactly once, and passes any other vertex at most once

constraints.name: velocity_extension_flow_conservation
constraints.indexed_over: train tr, intermediate vertex v, velocity-extension i
constraints.expression_latex: \sum_{e \in \delta^{in}(v)} y_{tr,e,*,i} = \sum_{e \in \delta^{out}(v)} y_{tr,e,i,*}
constraints.expression_plain: velocity continuity: the speed at which a train arrives at a vertex must equal the speed at which it leaves (flow conservation on velocity-extended arcs)

constraints.name: illegal_path_elimination
constraints.indexed_over: train tr, edge pairs (e,e2) that are not a valid successor
constraints.expression_latex: x_{tr,e} + x_{tr,e2} \leq 1
constraints.expression_plain: forbid traversing two consecutive edges that do not form a valid path (e.g. too-sharp turns)

constraints.name: edge_travel_time_bounds
constraints.indexed_over: train tr, edge e, feasible (i,j)
constraints.expression_latex: t^{front\_arrival}_{tr,target(e)} \geq t^{front\_departure}_{tr,source(e)} + t^{min}_{arc} - (M + t^{min}_{arc})(1 - y_{tr,e,i,j}); \quad t^{front\_arrival}_{tr,target(e)} \leq t^{front\_departure}_{tr,source(e)} + t^{max}_{arc} + (M - t^{max}_{arc})(1 - y_{tr,e,i,j})
constraints.expression_plain: if a velocity-extended arc is used, the front travel time over that edge lies between the kinematic minimum and maximum travel time (big-M deactivated otherwise; upper bound omitted when max travel time is infinite)

constraints.name: departure_after_arrival_and_stop_logic
constraints.indexed_over: train tr, vertex v
constraints.expression_latex: t^{front\_departure}_{tr,v} \geq t^{front\_arrival}_{tr,v}; \quad t^{front\_departure}_{tr,v} \leq t^{front\_arrival}_{tr,v} + M \cdot (\text{speed-0 arcs at } v)
constraints.expression_plain: departure is no earlier than arrival at a vertex, and equals it unless the train can come to a stop there (i.e. uses a zero-speed arc)

constraints.name: basic_edge_order
constraints.indexed_over: edge e, train pair (tr1,tr2) on e (non-lazy mode)
constraints.expression_latex: order_{tr1,tr2,e} + order_{tr2,tr1,e} \leq \tfrac12 (x_{tr1,e}+x_{tr2,e}); \quad order_{tr1,tr2,e}+order_{tr2,tr1,e} \geq x_{tr1,e}+x_{tr2,e}-1
constraints.expression_plain: if both trains use an edge, exactly one ordering is selected; if not both, none

constraints.name: train_rear_departure_definition
constraints.indexed_over: train tr, vertex v, brake/length paths p
constraints.expression_latex: t^{rear\_departure}_{tr,v} \geq t^{front\_departure}_{tr,\cdot} + (\text{min time for rear to clear length } len_{tr}) - M\,(\text{path not used})
constraints.expression_plain: the rear-departure time at v is derived from the front-departure time plus the time the train of length len_tr needs to fully clear v along each candidate path (handles trains partially leaving the network at the exit)

constraints.name: stopping_constraints
constraints.indexed_over: train tr, stop k, candidate vertex v
constraints.expression_latex: \sum_v stop_{tr,k,v} = 1; \; t^{front\_dep}_{tr,v} - t^{front\_arr}_{tr,v} \geq stoptime_k\, stop_{tr,k,v}; \; t_0^{min}\,stop \leq t^{front\_arr}_{tr,v} \leq t_0^{max} + M(1-stop); \; stop_{tr,k,v} \leq \sum_p path\_expr
constraints.expression_plain: each scheduled stop is served at exactly one candidate vertex; the dwell time meets the minimum stopping time; arrival and departure fall inside the timetable windows; a stop is allowed only if a valid edge path through it is used

constraints.name: initial_final_time_windows
constraints.indexed_over: train tr
constraints.expression_latex: t0^{min}_{tr} \leq t^{front\_arr}_{tr,entry} \leq t0^{max}_{tr}; \quad tmin\_n_{tr} \leq t^{rear\_dep}_{tr,exit} \leq tmax\_n_{tr}
constraints.expression_plain: the network entry and exit times of each train must lie inside the timetable's arrival/departure windows

constraints.name: headway_constraints_moving_block
constraints.indexed_over: train tr, vertex v, source velocity, brake path p, following train tr2
constraints.expression_latex: t^{front\_arrival}_{tr,v} + M(|p| - \sum_{e\in p} x_{tr,e}) + M(1 - order_{tr,tr2,p_{last}}) \geq t^{rear\_departure}_{tr2,\cdot} + (\text{kinematic terms})
constraints.expression_plain: core moving-block safety: a following train may only reach the moving-authority point (front position minus its braking distance) after the leading train's rear has cleared that point; the braking distance bd = v^2/(2 d_tr) defines the look-ahead path p along which the constraint is enforced

constraints.name: simplified_headway_constraints
constraints.indexed_over: train tr, edge e, following train tr2 (only if simplify_headway_constraints)
constraints.expression_latex: t^{front\_dep}_{tr,source(e)} - t^{rear\_dep}_{tr2,target(e)} + (M + hw_{max})(1 - order_{tr,tr2,e}) \geq hw_{tr,e}
constraints.expression_plain: less accurate but cheaper headway alternative defined per edge instead of per braking path; also enforced on entering TTD sections

constraints.name: basic_ttd_constraints
constraints.indexed_over: TTD section s, train tr, train pair (tr,tr2)
constraints.expression_latex: x^{ttd}_{tr,s} \geq x_{tr,e}\;(e\in s), \; x^{ttd}_{tr,s} \leq \sum_{e\in s} x_{tr,e}; \; t^{ttd\_dep}_{tr,s} \geq t^{rear\_dep}_{tr,target(e)} - M(1-x_{tr,e}); \; order\_ttd\text{ linking + time order}
constraints.expression_plain: a TTD section is used iff one of its edges is used; section-departure time is bounded by rear departure on used edges; only one train order per shared TTD section, with consistent departure-time ordering (TTD sections allow only one train at a time)

constraints.name: reverse_edge_constraints
constraints.indexed_over: reverse edge pair idx, opposite-direction train pair (tr1,tr2)
constraints.expression_latex: reverse\_order_{tr1,tr2}+reverse\_order_{tr2,tr1} \geq x_{tr1,e1}+x_{tr2,e2}-1, \;\leq 1; \quad t^{front\_arr}_{tr1,source} + M(1-reverse\_order_{tr1,tr2}) \geq t^{rear\_dep}_{tr2,source}
constraints.expression_plain: two trains traversing the same physical track in opposite directions must be temporally separated so they never meet

constraints.name: vertex_headway_constraints
constraints.indexed_over: edge e, train pair (tr1,tr2) sharing e
constraints.expression_latex: t^{front\_arr}_{tr1,source} + (M + hw^{s1}_{max})(1 - order_{tr1,tr2,e}) \geq t^{rear\_dep}_{tr2,source} + hw^{s1} \quad(\text{and analog at target vertex / both orders})
constraints.expression_plain: enforce any line headway specified at shared vertices (most importantly exit nodes), depending on the train order, at both endpoints of the shared edge

instances.name: example-networks (same 10 networks as the VSS model)
instances.format: directory per instance (network/, routes/, timetable/); GeneralPerformanceOptimizationInstance supports general timetables with time windows

models.notes: Constraints are assembled in create_constraints (GenPOMovingBlockMIPSolver.cpp:420-447). When solver_strategy.use_lazy_constraints is true (default), basic_order, vertex_headway, reverse_edge and headway/simplified_headway constraints are NOT added up front; instead they are generated on demand inside LazyCallback (GenPOMovingBlockMIPSolver_Lazy.cpp), with several selection strategies (OnlyViolated/OnlyFirstFound/AllChecked, OnlyAdjacent/All trains). The big-M for timing constraints is the per-train upper exit time tmax_n_tr (ub_timing_variable). Velocity-extension sets and kinematic feasibility (possible_by_eom, min/max_travel_time*) are computed in EOMHelper and fill_velocity_extensions (MinOneStep vs None strategy). A separate A*-based solver (GenPOMovingBlockAStarSolver) exists for the same problem but is NOT a MILP and is excluded. The detailed algebraic form of the headway and rear-departure kinematic coefficient terms is implemented numerically (helper functions) rather than as closed-form symbols; those coefficient expressions are summarized in expression_plain rather than transcribed term-by-term.

extraction_notes: Two distinct Gurobi MILP formulations were found and extracted. (1) VSS Generation Timetable solver: class VSSGenTimetableSolver across VSSGenTimetableSolver_general.cpp (variables/objective/general+non-discretized constraints), VSSGenTimetableSolver_fixedRoutes.cpp, and VSSGenTimetableSolver_freeRoutes.cpp; header VSSGenTimetableSolver.hpp. (2) GenPO Moving-Block routing solver: class GenPOMovingBlockMIPSolver in GenPOMovingBlockMIPSolver.cpp (+ _Lazy.cpp lazy callbacks). Counts: VSS model ~25 named decision-variable families and 21 constraint groups extracted (each group corresponds to one create_*_constraints method and may expand to many indexed Gurobi rows); GenPO model 11 variable families and 14 constraint groups. The repository has NO single README/paper section transcribing the equations, so symbols were invented from code identifiers and recorded in source_symbol; mathematical meaning was inferred from inline comments and variable names. paper_reference at the top level is null because the repo spans multiple papers ([3]-[6]) with no single canonical DOI; per-model DOIs are given in each model's description (VSS=ATMOS 2023 doi:10.4230/OASIcs.ATMOS.2023.6; GenPO=FedCSIS 2024 doi:10.15439/2024F3041 / arXiv:2405.18977). GAPS / things set to null or summarized rather than transcribed: (a) the exact big-M-laden kinematic coefficients in the VSS impossibility cuts and the GenPO headway/rear-departure constraints are computed by numeric helper functions (EOMHelper: min/max_travel_time, min_time_from_rear_to_ma_point, possible_by_eom) and are described in plain English rather than as closed-form LaTeX, to avoid fabricating formulas; (b) the brakelen constraint and Inferred-model PWL/product linearizations make the VSS model an MIQCP/MINLP-with-PWL under some settings, not a pure MILP; (c) the free-routes variant constraints (VSSGenTimetableSolver_freeRoutes.cpp) are summarized at the group level rather than enumerated row-by-row given their volume; (d) lazy-constraint generation logic in GenPOMovingBlockMIPSolver_Lazy.cpp mirrors the up-front headway/order/reverse constraints and was not separately enumerated. All source_files line ranges were read directly during extraction. Nothing was invented beyond mapping code identifiers to math symbols.
